name: Mariquita's Enchilada Red Chili Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry the chilies in a little of the oil until they are lightly browned. Remove and soak in hot water for 25 to 30 minutes.
2. Drain the chilies, reserving 1 cup of the soaking liquid.
3. Remove the seeds from the chilies and combine them with the onion, reserved liquid, roasted tomato, garlic, cumin, and salt in a blender container.
4. Blend until smooth.
5. Pour the mixture back into a pan with a little oil and cook over medium heat for about 10 minutes.
6. Use this gravy with enchiladas or with shredded or thinly sliced roast pork. It makes enough to serve about 12 enchiladas.
7. To make enchiladas, spread grated Chihuahua or Monterey Jack cheese on corn tortillas and roll them up tightly. Place seam side down in a casserole dish, pour the heated gravy over the enchiladas, and heat in a 400-degree oven for about 10 minutes. Top with finely chopped onion and Mexican sour cream.
This is Mariquita Masterson's version of the red chili gravy traditionally served with enchiladas. Heat the sauce before using.
